title: Simple Makeup Vanity with Shelf Hutch
summary: A slim two-piece makeup vanity — a plank-top table with a small open hutch on the back for products. Sized around your own light-up mirror.
description:
This is a two-part build that reads as one piece: a simple plank-top table for the base, and a small open-shelf hutch that sits on the back edge to hold products and frame a lighted mirror. Keeping them separate is what makes it approachable — the base is structural and gets pocket-hole strength, while the hutch is light decor and can be brad-nailed together, no fussy joinery.

The base is about as simple as a table gets: two wide 1x12 legs hung under a 1x12 top with a half-inch overhang, tied together with a pair of 1x4 aprons. Measure the leg spacing at the top for your apron length rather than trusting the drawing — a 1x12's real width varies and the aprons have to fit what you actually built.

The light-up mirror itself is a purchased item, not part of the build; size the hutch opening to the mirror you're using. This is a great early furniture project precisely because the hard part (the base) is honest and square, and the forgiving part (the hutch) is where a beginner's small errors hide.

tools.note: The base's structural joints; the hutch can skip it.
The hutch is glued and pinned — easier to hide than screws.

materials.name: Light-up mirror
materials.note: Purchased. Size the hutch opening to fit it — not part of the woodworking.

cutList.note: Cut to the measured distance between the legs at the tabletop.

steps.title: Hang the legs under the top
steps.body: Drill three pocket holes on one end of each 1x12 leg board. Attach them to the underside of the top with 1-1/4" pocket screws, leaving a 1/2" overhang on each end. Keep the legs square to the top.

steps.title: Add the aprons
steps.body: Measure the distance between the two legs at the tabletop for the most accurate number, and cut the two 1x4 aprons to it. Drill two 3/4" pocket holes at each end, plus pocket holes along one edge of the top apron facing up to attach it to the tabletop. Fasten with 1-1/4" pocket screws. This ties the legs together and stiffens the base.

steps.title: Build and mount the hutch
steps.body:
Build the hutch with brad nails and glue — it isn't structural, so nails are fine and hide better than pocket screws. Build the two shelf sections first, then add the top. Size the center opening to your light-up mirror. Connect the finished hutch to the base with a few screws through the back.

Sand and finish the whole piece.

images.alt: A simple makeup vanity table with a small open shelf hutch and a lighted mirror
